title: IPOs should be approached with caution, Apollo top economist says
excerpt: SpaceX has struggled since its debut, and history shows that offerings since 2019 have underperformed in the years since.
content_html:
One chart shows why investors may want to approach this year's mega IPOs with caution

By Samuel O'Brient

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

The AI hype seems endless, and investors are salivating at the chance to finally buy stock in some of the biggest names in the industry.

But as Anthropic and OpenAI prepare to go public, and as SpaceX stock struggles after a historic IPO, there's reason for investors to tread carefully, Apollo's top economist, Torsten Sløk, says.

SpaceX kicked off the summer with the largest IPO ever, setting the stage for this year's deluge of stock offerings from some of the most coveted private tech companies.

But as a chart shared by Sløk shows, recent history suggests the stock of newly public companies is risky bet in the years following an IPO.

Since 2019, the market regime that's influence the post-IPO stock performance of companies has been characterized by three things: "peak valuations," "a hostile rate regime," and "low quality, high bar," Sløk said.

"The boom pushed marginal companies public before they were ready while the market-adjusted benchmark was set against an index carried by a handful of mega-cap winners," he wrote.

The economist highlighted that this was particularly true during the pandemic-era boom of 2020 and 2021. The combination of zero interest rates and high retail demand fueled by government stimulus created an environment that sparked rich valuation for companies rushing to go public.

Now a new wave of mega-IPOs is taking shape, as OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to debut. Despite some concern about their high valuations, investors excitement is high.

Other market pros have raised similar concerns about the rush to public markets. Economist and IPO-market veteran Jay Ritter told Business Insider that history suggested that SpaceX stock would underperform. His call has proven accurate about six weeks after the offering, with the stock down the stock down about 18% from the $135 IPO price.

"Each of these forces could persist," Sløk added of the factors that hobbled the IPOs in the years after 2019. "Valuations may re-inflate in the next IPO window, rates look set to stay structurally higher than the 2010s and index returns remain concentrated in a few mega-caps that keep the relative bar high."

Samuel O'Brient

His reporting has been cited by The New York Times DealBook, Bloomberg Radio, Forbes, Entrepreneur, Gizmodo and TheFutureParty.Samuel began at InvestorPlace, covering investing, retail trading and macro economic trends. Prior to joining Business Insider,  he served as a technology markets reporter at TheStreet. content_html_zh:
一张图表显示了为什么投资者可能希望谨慎对待今年的大型首次公开募股

作者：Samuel O'Brient 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

人工智能的炒作似乎永无休止，投资者对最终购买业内一些知名企业股票的机会垂涎欲滴。

但阿波罗的首席经济学家 Torsten Sløk 表示，随着 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 准备上市，以及 SpaceX 股票在历史性 IPO 后陷入困境，投资者有理由谨慎行事。

SpaceX 以史上最大规模的 IPO 拉开了今年夏天的序幕，为今年一些最令人垂涎​​的私营科技公司大量发行股票奠定了基础。

但正如 Sløk 分享的图表所示，最近的历史表明，新上市公司的股票在首次公开募股后的几年里是有风险的押注。

Sløk 表示，自 2019 年以来，影响公司 IPO 后股票表现的市场机制具有三个特点：“估值峰值”、“敌意的利率机制”和“低质量、高标准”。

他写道：“繁荣迫使边缘公司在准备好之前上市，而经过市场调整的基准是针对少数大型股赢家所持有的指数设定的。”

这位经济学家强调，在 2020 年和 2021 年大流行时期的繁荣时期尤其如此。 零利率和政府刺激推动的高零售需求相结合，创造了一个环境，为急于上市的公司带来了丰厚的估值。

现在，随着 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 准备首次亮相，新一波大型 IPO 正在形成。 尽管有人担心它们的高估值，但投资者的兴奋程度很高。

其他市场专业人士也对涌入公开市场提出了类似的担忧。 经济学家兼 IPO 市场资深人士 Jay Ritter 告诉 Business Insider，历史表明 SpaceX 股票的表现将会不佳。 事实证明，在发行大约六周后，他的预测是准确的，股价较 135 美元的 IPO 价格下跌了约 18%。

在谈到 2019 年之后的几年中阻碍 IPO 的因素时，Sløk 补充道：“这些力量都可能持续存在。” “估值可能会在下一个 IPO 窗口中重新膨胀，利率看起来将在结构上保持高于 2010 年代的水平，而指数回报仍集中在少数大型股上，从而保持相对较高的水平。”

塞缪尔·奥布莱恩 (Samuel O'Brient) 是一位经验丰富的金融市场和商业记者，撰写的文章涉及经济、技术和公共政策等广泛主题。 在《商业内幕》中，他报道了重要的宏观和微观经济故事，包括顶尖经济学家和对冲基金经理的观点、首次公开募股失败、企业破产、迷因股票和卖空。 他还撰写有关加密货币、石油和房地产等其他市场的文章。他采访了许多市场上最有影响力的声音，其中包括马克·赞迪（Mark Zandi）和理查德·泰勒托（Richard Thalerto）等顶级经济学家，以及丹尼·摩西（Danny Moses）、安德鲁·莱夫特（Andrew Left）、安东尼·斯卡拉穆奇（Anthony Scaramucci）、路易斯·纳维利尔（Louis Navellier）和格兰特·卡登（Grant Cardone）等著名投资者。福克斯的 LiveNOW、Taking Stock 和 Ticker News 等节目都邀请塞缪尔讨论股市和经济发展。 他的报道曾被纽约时报 DealBook、彭博电台、福布斯、Entrepreneur、Gizmodo 和 TheFutureParty 引用。Samuel 最初在 InvestorPlace 工作，报道投资、零售交易和宏观经济趋势。 在加入 Business Insider 之前，他曾担任 TheStreet 的技术市场记者。 他毕业于莎拉·劳伦斯学院和都柏林三一学院。Samuel 的作品曾发表在 TipRanks、EV 和 Observer 等出版物上。 当他不追寻故事时，人们经常会发现他在逛书店和唱片店。 要联系 Samuel，请发送电子邮件至 sobrient@insider.com 或通过 LinkedIn 与他联系。 他还在《信号》中饰演塞缪尔·克莱门斯。 热门文章： 一位诺贝尔经济学家向 meme 股票交易员发出警告 发起 Beyond Meat 集会的商学院辍学者希望你知道他不是 Roaring Kitty 2.0 一位认为我们正处于衰退边缘的顶级经济学家表示，他正在关注这 3 个警告信号 特朗普的 401(k) 行政命令标志着退休储蓄的重大变化 - 可能会让你的钱面临风险 为什么对冲基金偶像雷·达利奥 (Ray Dalio) 说你不应该投资房地产在当前经济形势下，人工智能的乐观情绪正在飙升，但专业人士看到了市场中被忽视的角落正在酝酿的重大机遇
